q: In the spoken sentence, 'I didn't say *he* stole the money,' stressing the word 'he' implies that:
opts: I don't think any money was stolen.
Someone else may have stolen the money.
I said he stole something else.
I am certain he is innocent.

opts: To show you have a better vocabulary
To confirm your understanding of the message
To change the subject of discussion

opts: 'It sounds like you have too much on your plate right now.'

q: Which of the following questions is an 'open question' designed to encourage a speaker to share more details?
opts: 'What was your favorite part of the trip?'

opts: A request for a movie recommendation

opts: I stole a tablet, not a laptop.

opts: Receiving too much data at once to process
